reading.title: Reading: Cause, result, and contrast
reading.passage: I was late because the traffic was heavy. It rained heavily, so we stayed at home. Although it was expensive, she bought the dress. The room was small, but it was comfortable. However, we still enjoyed our stay.

questions.question: The writer was late because of the weather.
questions.explanation: Lý do là "the traffic was heavy", không phải thời tiết.

questions.question: Why did she buy the dress?
options: Although it was expensive, she liked it
questions.correct: Although it was expensive, she liked it
questions.explanation: "Although it was expensive, she bought the dress."

reading.title: Reading: Review — Rules, advice, and reasons
reading.passage: You must wear a helmet, and you shouldn't ride too fast. You don't have to wear a uniform, but you have to arrive on time. It might rain, so take an umbrella. I enjoy walking to work because it is too far to drive but not too far to walk.

questions.question: Wearing a uniform is compulsory.
questions.explanation: "You don't have to wear a uniform".

questions.question: The writer enjoys walking to work.
